meta.title: San Martin's Bridge — Toledo, Spain
meta.description: Complete guide to San Martin's Bridge: 14th-century pedestrian bridge with spectacular Tagus River views, Gothic architecture and medieval history.

hero.title: San Martin's Bridge
hero.subtitle: 14th-century pedestrian bridge with sweeping views of the Tagus River

about.title: Discover the Bridge
about.description: San Martin's Bridge, built in the 14th century, is a medieval architectural jewel spanning the Tagus River in Toledo. This pedestrian bridge offers spectacular views of the walled city and the natural Tagus landscape, serving as a living testament to Spanish history.

highlights.title: Highlights
items: Panoramic views of the Tagus River and Toledo city
Ideal photography spot to capture Toledo's essence
Historic connection between Tagus River banks

about.officialManagement: Puente de San Martín is jointly managed and protected by the Ayuntamiento de Toledo and the regional government of Castilla-La Mancha. As an important historical and cultural heritage of Toledo, this bridge is classified as a 'Bien de Interés Cultural' (Property of Cultural Interest) in Spain.

items.title: Medieval Architecture
items.description: Admire the impressive Gothic architecture of the bridge with its five arches of different sizes. The medieval construction demonstrates the mastery of 14th-century architects and their ability to adapt to the rocky Tagus terrain.

items.title: Panoramic Tagus River Views
items.description: From the bridge you can enjoy the best views of the Tagus River and its meander that surrounds the city of Toledo. The natural landscape perfectly combines with the historic architecture of the city.

items.title: Photography and Viewpoints
items.description: San Martin's Bridge is an ideal spot for photography, offering multiple angles to capture Toledo's beauty. Sunrise and sunset provide perfect lighting for spectacular photos.

items.title: Connection with History
items.description: Walking across this bridge transports you to medieval times. It has witnessed centuries of Toledan history and continues to be a vital element of the city's cultural heritage.

gallery.title: Photo Gallery
gallery.subtitle: Images of the bridge and surroundings
gallery.viewAll: View all photos on Google Maps

images.caption: Medieval bridge view

reviews.title: Real Visitor Reviews
reviews.declaration: Ratings and reviews from Google Maps (last updated: 2026). We show only a selection of verified high-rated reviews. To see all complete and most recent reviews, click the link below.
reviews.seeAll: See all reviews on Google Maps

items.name: Ana Martínez
items.text: Impressive medieval bridge with spectacular views of the Tagus River. Perfect for photos and peaceful walks. The Gothic architecture is stunning.

items.name: Javier Rodríguez
items.text: A historical gem in Toledo. The views from the bridge are magnificent, especially at sunset. Well worth the visit.

items.name: Laura Fernández
items.text: Beautiful 14th-century bridge. Walking across it transports you to medieval times. The views of the Tagus and city are unforgettable.

items.name: Manuel García
items.text: Excellent example of medieval architecture. The perfect place to contemplate Toledo's natural beauty and the Tagus River. Highly recommended.

items.name: Carmen López
items.text: Toledo wouldn't be the same without this bridge. The panoramic views are spectacular and the history it holds is fascinating.

items.name: Roberto Sánchez
items.text: Very well-preserved medieval bridge. Perfect for photography and enjoying Tagus views. A must-visit in Toledo.

items.name: Isabel Gómez
items.text: Wonderful testament to medieval engineering. The natural Tagus setting makes it even more special. Don't miss it.

items.name: Francisco Pérez
items.text: The views from the bridge are stunning. The combination of history, architecture, and nature makes it a unique place.

hours.label: Opening Hours
hours.value: Pedestrian access is free (Acceso peatonal gratuito). Open 24 hours a day. No tickets or reservations are required. We highly recommend visiting at sunset to enjoy panoramic views of the Tagus River (Río Tajo) and the old city of Toledo.

transport.label: Getting There
transport.value: From Madrid: 45 min by high-speed train or 1 hour by car. From Toledo station: 20 min walk or 10 min by bus.

parking.label: Parking
parking.value: Free parking available nearby. In peak season (September-May) it can be limited — arrive early recommended.

accessibility.label: Accessibility
accessibility.value: Pedestrian access with ramps available. Bridge has uneven stone surface — comfortable footwear recommended.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This website is managed by the Ayuntamiento de Toledo and the regional government. We are committed to protecting visitor privacy and complying with the EU GDPR and Spanish data protection regulations.

sections.title: Information We Collect
sections.content: We generally only collect browsing data and necessary cookies. We do not use this information for commercial purposes.

sections.title: How We Use Your Information
sections.content: The collected information is used solely to improve the website experience and provide official tourist information.

sections.title: Data Sharing
sections.content: We will not sell or share your data with third-party commercial organizations. Information may only be shared with government departments as required by law.

sections.title: Your Rights
sections.content: Under applicable regulations, you have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal information.

sections.title: Third-Party Services
sections.content: This website uses Google Maps to provide geographical location, addresses, and navigation information. When you use these features, Google may collect your IP address and location data according to their own privacy policies. We do not control Google's data processing activities.

sections.title: Contact
sections.content: For privacy-related questions, please contact the official channels of the Ayuntamiento de Toledo or Turismo Toledo.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: This website is the official information platform for Puente de San Martín, providing public tourism information services. By using this website, you agree to these Terms of Service.

sections.title: Content Ownership
sections.content: All content on this site, including text and images, belongs to the Ayuntamiento de Toledo or is used under authorization.

sections.title: Disclaimer
sections.content: The information provided is for reference only. Please refer to the latest information from the official tourism board. We assume no responsibility for any consequences arising from the use of this information.

sections.title: Prohibited Actions
sections.content: Commercial abuse, unauthorized data scraping, and other malicious behaviors are strictly prohibited.

sections.title: Governing Law
sections.content: These terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Spain.

sections.title: External Links and Third-Party Services
sections.content: Our website contains links to external services, including Google Maps. These services are governed by their respective terms and privacy policies. The Ayuntamiento de Toledo assumes no responsibility for the content, privacy practices, or data processing of any third-party websites or services.

cookieSettings.title: Cookie Settings
cookieSettings.intro: We use cookies to ensure the proper functioning of the website. We respect your privacy and comply with GDPR requirements.

necessary.title: Essential Cookies
necessary.description: Required for the website to function properly (e.g., language selection, session maintenance).

analytics.title: Analytics Cookies
analytics.description: We may use analytics cookies to understand visitor usage (anonymized, no personal identifiable information collected). This includes cookies set by third-party services like Google Maps to provide mapping functionalities.

preferences.title: Preference Cookies
preferences.description: Remember your settings such as language and theme preferences.

marketing.title: Marketing Cookies
marketing.description: Used to deliver relevant advertisements and track campaign effectiveness.
